goal: Drift monitoring: scheduled runs, engagement-level summary endpoint, notification triggers for critical drift, and drift executor registration

acceptance_criteria.description: DriftSchedule model in models/types.go: schedule_id (UUID), engagement_id, interval_hours (int, min 1, max 168), enabled (bool), last_triggered_at (timestamptz), next_trigger_at (timestamptz), created_at, updated_at. Migration 053_drift_schedule.sql creates migration.drift_schedule with UNIQUE(engagement_id) — one schedule per engagement. RLS Tier B. Default interval_hours=24, enabled=false.

POST /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/drift-schedule creates or updates the drift schedule. Request body: {interval_hours, enabled}. Uses INSERT ON CONFLICT (engagement_id) DO UPDATE — idempotent upsert. Validates engagement is in GO_LIVE status — returns 409 if not. This GO_LIVE restriction applies only to scheduled drift; the existing one-off manual drift detection endpoint (POST /drift-detection from M05a) remains unrestricted by engagement status. GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/drift-schedule returns the current schedule (or 404 if none). PATCH /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/drift-schedule updates interval_hours and/or enabled. Returns 200 with updated schedule. When enabled transitions false to true, next_trigger_at is set to NOW() + interval_hours.

DriftSchedulerLoop in worker/drift_scheduler.go: background goroutine (started in main.go alongside StaleRecoveryLoop). Polls migration.drift_schedule every 5 minutes for rows WHERE enabled = true AND next_trigger_at <= NOW(). For each due schedule: creates a drift detection run (drift_type='BOTH'), enqueues drift_detection job, updates last_triggered_at = NOW() and next_trigger_at = NOW() + interval_hours. Uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to prevent duplicate triggers across multiple API instances. Respects context cancellation for graceful shutdown.

GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/drift-summary returns a DriftSummary: last_run_at (timestamp of most recent completed run), last_run_id, total_runs (int), drift_status ('CLEAN'|'DRIFTED'|'CRITICAL' — based on most recent completed run: CLEAN if 0 detected_changes, CRITICAL if critical_changes > 0, DRIFTED otherwise), by_severity (map[string]int — CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW counts from most recent run's records), schedule_enabled (bool), next_scheduled_at (nullable timestamp). Single query joins drift_detection_run and drift_schedule for efficiency.

CreateNotification function in db/notification.go: CreateNotification(db *sql.DB, tenantID, engagementID, engagementName, notifType, summary string) (*models.Notification, error). Inserts into migration.notification and returns the created record. Notification trigger in drift executor: when a drift detection run completes with critical_changes > 0, call CreateNotification with type='DRIFT_CRITICAL', summary='Critical drift detected: {N} schema changes affecting mapped fields in {engagement_name}'. WebSocket broadcast: notification_created with the notification record — NotificationBell component can auto-refresh.

Drift executor registration: worker.DriftDetectionExecutor (already implemented in worker/drift_executor.go from M05a) must be registered in main.go alongside existing L1/L2/ParallelRun executors. The executor was created by M05a but never registered. Verify: after registration, enqueuing a drift_detection job via the job queue results in the DriftDetectionExecutor being invoked. If already registered, verify it functions correctly with the DriftSchedulerLoop by running a scheduled drift detection end-to-end.

critical: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED on drift_schedule — prevents duplicate drift triggers across API replicas
Drift schedule only allowed on GO_LIVE engagements — consistent with production monitoring use case
CreateNotification is generic and reusable — not drift-specific, other contracts (M11a) will use it
RLS Tier B on drift_schedule — tenant isolation enforced
Drift executor registered in main.go — fills gap from M05a where executor was created but not registered
high: Scheduler loop polls every 5 minutes — not per-second, to minimize DB load
drift_status badge derived from most recent COMPLETED run — not cumulative across all runs
UNIQUE(engagement_id) on drift_schedule — one schedule per engagement, upsert pattern
WebSocket broadcast on notification creation — NotificationBell auto-refresh
medium: interval_hours clamped to 1-168 (1 hour to 1 week) — prevents abuse
DriftSummary endpoint joins two tables in one query — no N+1
Scheduler respects context cancellation — clean shutdown in main.go
